title: $variation
text: $variation Return or specify the name of the library containing objects needed by the application or component. $variation $variation = String Example: $variation = "ACME" Return Values Name of the current library. Use Allowed in Form, Report, and Service components, but not in self-contained components. Description The value of $variation determines the library used to locate the following resources at runtime: Messages Help texts Menus Panels Glyphs Device translation tables Keyboard translation tables Language setups It overrides the value set by the $VARIATION assignment setting, but not the library specified by the Library property of the application shell or component. This cannot be changed without recompiling the component or application shell. Changing the value of $variation does not affect the global ProcScript and global variables used at application run time. Related Links $VARIATION Specify the library name for global objects needed by the application when the application starts. Libraries are typed, so each global object library can have the same name which functions as a shared namespace which can be specified by this setting. $language Return or set the current language code used for language-related global objects and language setups.
